---
title: "Curriculum Vitae"
---

## Nancy (Nanxi) Lu

+44 7579 958 424 | [Nanxiliam@hotmail.com](mailto:Nanxiliam@hotmail.com) | [nanxi.lu.25@ucl.ac.uk](mailto:nanxi.lu.25@ucl.ac.uk) | [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nanxilunae2003)

## Profile

I am an urban planning and spatial data student interested in city regeneration, transport planning, urban spatial analysis and evidence-led design. My work combines planning knowledge with digital and analytical tools, including CAD, SketchUp, Photoshop, Illustrator, ArcGIS, QGIS, SQL, R, Python, NetLogo, machine learning and data mining.

I have a strong understanding of UK planning systems, development processes and site assessment, with experience producing clear recommendations for practical and operational decisions. I am a diligent, adaptable and collaborative learner, with experience coordinating teamwork and contributing within multidisciplinary planning, design and transport environments.

## Education

[Browse my coursework ->](coursework.qmd)

### MSc Urban Spatial Science, University College London, London, UK | September 2025 - September 2026

- Royal Geographical Society with IBG accredited.
- Core modules include Geographic Information Systems and Science, Data Science for Spatial Systems, Building Spatial Applications with Big Data, Spatial-Temporal Data Analysis and Data Mining, and Agent-Based Modelling.
- Developing skills in spatial data analysis, geographic information systems, data science and urban modelling to analyse, visualise and apply complex spatial datasets to real-world urban challenges.

### BSc Urban Planning and Development (Hons), Cardiff University, Cardiff, UK | September 2022 - July 2025

- RICS and RTPI accredited.
- Awarded the Vice-Chancellor's Scholarship for entry.
- Dissertation: *A Comparative Study of Regeneration in Cardiff Bay and Durban Port*.
- Relevant modules include Urban Regeneration, Sustainable Environment and Developing Research Methods.
- Built a strong foundation in British planning legislation, sustainability principles, urban policy, site assessment, development processes and evidence-based planning.

### National Senior Certificate, The Wykeham Collegiate, Pietermaritzburg, South Africa | January 2017 - December 2021

- South African IEB syllabus, equivalent to AAA at A-Level.
- Top 100 in KwaZulu-Natal for the National Mathematics Olympiad.
- Silver Medal for the National English Olympiad.
- Bowman-Edgar Trophy for hard work and excellence.

## Experience

### Assistant Transport Analyst, Liverpool City Region Combined Authority, Liverpool, UK | May 2026 - Present

- Support the monitoring and evaluation of Liverpool City Region transport schemes by analysing how usage changes after new infrastructure is delivered.

### Transport Planning Intern, U.A Design China Ltd., Shanghai, China | September 2024 - November 2024

- Assisted planning and architecture teams on design projects in Shanghai during a remote role.
- Prepared development surveying reports based on regulatory planning documents.
- Produced transport planning maps and contributed to project presentations.

### Planning Intern, Tus Design Group Ltd., Suzhou, China | June 2024 - August 2024

- Contributed to tourism planning, industrial park planning and community street regeneration projects.
- Supported master planning work through research, 3D modelling, preliminary rendering and investment recommendations.
- Used ArcGIS and regulatory maps to design proposed upgrades for outdated facilities in the Suzhou Pingjiang District Regeneration project.
- Conducted resident surveys and contributed ethnographic research to support local regeneration recommendations.
- Collaborated with Architecture and BIM teams to integrate spatial-temporal data into a regulatory master plan for an Inner Mongolia industrial park, aligned with environmental sustainability legislation.
- Contributed to work that won the Suzhou Urban and Rural Construction System Outstanding Survey and Design Urban Planning Second Prize in 2024.
- Conducted historical research for a tourism project, joined site visits and drone-supported fieldwork, and helped develop project proposals.

### Planning Intern, Jinghailang Planning Institute, Nantong, China | June 2023 - August 2023

- Worked on the Qidong Ancient City tourism development project by researching, building an evaluation system and assessing cultural tourism potential.
- Contributed to a project ranked in the Top 10 of the Jiangsu Province Tourism Development Project in 2023.

## Leadership and Achievements

- Vice Chair of the School Student Staff Panel, coordinating meetings between academic staff and student representatives and synthesising feedback on teaching and campus experience.
- Academic Representative in Year One, acting as a formal liaison between students and the school.
- Founding Secretary of the Cardiff University Digital Urbanist Society, supporting society setup and administration.
- Head of Culture and Head of Strings Ensemble in high school.
- IEMA Foundation Certificate in Environmental Management Level 3, completed in January 2025.

## Skills

### Spatial Analysis, Data and Planning Tools

- ArcGIS, QGIS, Google Earth Engine, SatScan, SNAP and NetLogo.
- Python, R, SQL, SPSS, machine learning and data mining.
- AutoCAD, SketchUp, Blender, Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator and InDesign.
- Microsoft Word, PowerPoint and Excel for reporting, presentations and data handling.
- Git and GitHub.

### Languages

- Mandarin Chinese and Shanghainese: native.
- English: bilingual proficiency.
- French: B2 proficiency.
- Japanese: A2 working knowledge.
- German: A1 basic knowledge.

## Volunteering

### CityPedia - Sustainable Transportation | January 2023 - Present

- Translator and proofreader, translating academic research and industry news for non-English readers.
- Connected with global built environment and transport professionals through a volunteer network of more than 1,000 members.

### Planning Aid Wales | December 2024 - August 2025

- Guided local communities and individuals through the Welsh planning system.
- Assisted with training sessions on planning legislation and best practice.
- Conducted policy research, prepared briefing documents and helped refine outreach strategies for community engagement.
- Maintained records to support timely follow-up and issue resolution.

## Music and Interests

- Piano: Trinity College London Grade 8 and South African National Eisteddfod Gold Medal.
- Keyboard player and music arranger for Cardiff University Orchestra.
- Violin: ABRSM Grade 6 and former concertmaster in high school's ensemble.
- Interests include tennis, running, hiking, pilates, painting, baking and travelling.

[Learn more about me ->](about.qmd)
